The use of gambling-like features, such as loot boxes in video games, is particularly troubling.

paragraphs.text: Dear Commission, I recently became aware of the Digital Fairness Act and would like to express my support for the initiative and the reasons why I believe it is essential to implement such legislation. As a consumer who has grown up immersed in the digital world, I have witnessed a significant shift in how online services are structured often to the detriment of user experience and consumer rights. What was once an open and empowering space has, in many cases, become an environment designed primarily to maximize profit, often at the expense of transparency, fairness, and user well-being. I would like to comment on several key points raised in the Act and share how they reflect real consumer experiences: Dark patterns Interface designs that intentionally manipulate users, for example by creating false urgency, are now commonplace. A clear example is the widespread use of fear of missing out (FOMO) mechanics in video games, such as time-limited battle passes, which pressure players into spending money. Addictive design Many digital services incorporate mechanics that encourage compulsive usage or spending. The use of gambling-like features, such as loot boxes in video games, is particularly troubling. These are often accessible to minors and can result in real financial loss for families. Exploitative personalisation Personalised advertising can take advantage of users' psychological or financial vulnerabilities. This raises serious concerns about privacy and informed consent, as companies continue to find increasingly opaque ways to track users across platforms and target them with emotionally manipulative content. Unfair subscription practices Subscriptions are frequently designed to be easy to start but intentionally difficult to cancel. In one instance, I had to contact multiple customer service representatives to cancel a subscription I never knowingly agreed to. This kind of design undermines user autonomy. Influencer marketing While I have limited experience with this area, I understand the importance of regulating undisclosed commercial content and protecting vulnerable audiences, especially minors, from harmful promotions. Overall, I strongly agree with the direction of this initiative. The proposed measures are timely and necessary to restore a sense of fairness, trust, and respect in the digital marketplace. However, I believe it is crucial that the implementation of this legislation is carefully balanced and clearly regulated. Any attempt to control the digital space must be done transparently and proportionately. At the first sign of an overreach that restricts legitimate freedoms or innovation, the publics perception of such regulation could shift dramatically and justifiably toward resistance. It is vital that this initiative protects both consumers and the integrity of the digital ecosystem. Thank you for the opportunity to share my views.
